title: The 25 for ’25 Selection Show Special
format: A planning meeting turned into a podcast: Sean and Amanda argue their way to a ranked list of the 25 best movies of the last 25 years, recorded in March 2025 before the list went public. One film per director, US releases from January 2000 on.

segments.heading: Building the long list
segments.note: the contenders, the alternates, the hard cuts

films.title: The Master
films.note: The Paul Thomas Anderson runner-up to There Will Be Blood.

films.title: No Country for Old Men
films.note: On Sean's list; 'not a very Coen-y movie,' he says, so it lost the Coen slot.

films.title: A Serious Man
films.note: Amanda's preferred Coen, alongside Inside Llewyn Davis.

films.title: Zodiac
films.note: Sean's favorite Fincher; 'the hardest cut on the whole list' under the one-per-director rule.

films.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood
films.note: Sean's Tarantino alternative to Inglourious Basterds, an asterisk he agonized over.

films.title: Moonlight
films.note: A hard cut; 'couldn't find a place for it' in the 25.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: The Gerwig alternative to Lady Bird: 'one of the most under-celebrated movies of the last 10 years.'

films.title: Nope
films.note: Amanda floated it as the Peele pick; Sean stayed with Get Out.

films.title: Lost in Translation
films.note: The 'should-be' Coppola pick they both wrote down before landing on Marie Antoinette.

films.title: Frances Ha
films.note: 'A no-brainer,' cut because Lady Bird already covered the Gerwig/Baumbach axis.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: The other Baumbach in play.

films.title: The Squid and the Whale
films.note: Sean's most personal Baumbach.

films.title: Greenberg
films.note: Briefly agreed as the Baumbach before Frances Ha, then dropped.

films.title: The Departed
films.note: Scorsese's most-seen of the century, but not their pick.

films.title: Silence
films.note: 'Will have its time on this show.'

films.title: Memories of Murder
films.note: No. 2 on their Bong Joon-ho ranking; 'the cool move.'

films.title: The Intern
films.note: The Nancy Meyers also-ran to Something's Gotta Give.

films.title: Oldboy
films.note: The other Park Chan-wook; Sean keeps threatening to swap it in for The Handmaiden.

films.title: Boyhood
films.note: The Linklater Amanda would have nudged Sean toward.

films.title: Everybody Wants Some!!
films.note: Sean's Linklater: 'the most fun movie.'

films.title: Y Tu Mamá También
films.note: The Cuarón pick on the list until Children of Men took the slot at the end.

films.title: Roma
films.note: 'Completely shredded' Sean in 2018; unrevisited since.

films.title: Gravity
films.note: 'A very stand-in script.'

films.title: The Act of Killing
films.note: Amanda's documentary pick, made on date night in Palm Springs.

films.title: The Fog of War
films.note: Errol Morris; Sean's 'red circle' among the docs, though no doc made the final 25.

films.title: O.J.: Made in America
films.note: Won the Documentary Feature Oscar, so eligible; Sean is thanked in the credits.

films.title: All the Beauty and the Bloodshed
Superbad
films.note: The comedy pick before they pivoted to Anchorman; a hard cut.

films.title: Step Brothers
films.note: 'The cool pick' vs. Anchorman's 'true pick.'

films.title: Whiplash
films.note: A hard cut; 'my most vulnerable rewatchables.'

films.title: First Man
films.note: 'The real cool-guy' Chazelle pick.

films.title: Tár
films.note: A day from a Cate Blanchett Hall of Fame taping; wanted it high, couldn't fit it.

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: On the shortlist at No. 21 for most of the meeting, cut late for The Tree of Life: 'the Tree of Life is better than the Dark Knight.'

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: 'Teetering on the edge'; Sean's blockbuster of the decade, cut for saminess.

films.title: Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon
films.note: The Ang Lee candidate; couldn't find a spot on the cut-down list.

films.title: Brokeback Mountain
films.note: Amanda flags early it won't make it; a real shame to Sean.

films.title: Inside Man
films.note: Sean's Spike Lee runner-up to 25th Hour: 'and it gets you Denzel.'

films.title: The Lost City of Z
films.note: Sean's James Gray pick; no Gray on the list.

films.title: The Devil Wears Prada
films.note: An Amanda-on-cocktails pick; 'hugely important to me.'

films.title: Margaret
films.note: The Lonergan hard cut; Sean lost his out-of-print Blu-ray to a former friend.

films.title: Hereditary
films.note: One of Amanda's picks under 'are we being too cute?'

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: The Safdie contender that fell out of the top 25.

films.title: Midnight in Paris
films.note: 'Riddled with controversy'; an episode would be a pain.

films.title: Amour
films.note: One of three Haneke films Sean wrote down.

films.title: The New World
films.note: 'The Malick heads love that one.'

films.title: Shaun of the Dead
films.note: 'Very big-picture movies': self-aware without sacrificing craft.

films.title: Yi Yi
films.note: Edward Yang, called one of the defining names of the century.

films.title: Pan's Labyrinth
films.note: Del Toro's most celebrated, per Sean.

films.title: Sully
films.note: The Eastwood both would pick: 'I like Sully a lot.'

films.title: Meek's Cutoff
films.note: Sean's Kelly Reichardt, over Wendy and Lucy.

films.title: The Assassination of Jesse James by the Coward Robert Ford
films.note: 'Directed by your fave, Andrew Dominik.'

films.title: Hunger
films.note: Sean's Steve McQueen over 12 Years a Slave.

films.title: Sicario
films.note: Sean's Villeneuve pick; Amanda leaned Arrival.

films.title: Arrival
films.note: A hard cut; no Villeneuve made the list.

films.title: The Lobster
films.note: Their shared Lanthimos.

films.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
films.note: 'Now like the Sight & Sound' consensus pick.

films.title: The Raid
films.note: 'Much like Oldboy, incredibly influential.'

films.title: John Wick
films.note: Sean's pick over John Wick 4.

films.title: Burning
films.note: Sean's No. 2 of its year.

films.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
films.note: 'Didn't make the long list, but that is cool.'

films.title: WALL·E
films.note: Sean's Pixar pick; no Pixar made the list.

films.title: It's Such a Beautiful Day
films.note: Don Hertzfeldt; 'big one for me.'

films.title: Call Me by Your Name
films.note: Sean's Guadagnino; Amanda answered with A Bigger Splash.

films.title: Almost Famous
films.note: Cut to make room for There Will Be Blood: 'sorry to Cameron Crowe.'

films.title: A.I. Artificial Intelligence
films.note: One of the missing Spielbergs.

films.title: Minority Report
films.note: 'Too much Cruise, too much action, too samey.'

films.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
films.note: 'What Severance is doing is so deeply riffing on' it.

films.title: Birth
films.note: Their Jonathan Glazer, if there were one.

films.title: The Hurt Locker
films.note: No Kathryn Bigelow on the list.

films.title: The Fabelmans
films.note: 'A case for it,' but not this list's conceit.

films.title: Certified Copy
films.note: 'Really important and good'; a Kiarostami they'd love to take apart.

films.title: Hero
films.note: Zhang Yimou.

segments.heading: The 25 for ’25 shortlist
segments.note: where it stood at the end of the meeting, No. 1 first
segments.subject: movies of 2000–2025

films.title: The Social Network
films.note: No. 1: 'our generation's Citizen Kane'; Fincher, over Zodiac.

films.title: 25th Hour
films.note: No. 2: the Spike Lee entry, over Inside Man. 'The greatest ending of a movie.'

films.title: There Will Be Blood
films.note: No. 3: the PTA pick over The Master; nearly left off by accident.

films.title: Inglourious Basterds
films.note: No. 4: Sean's Tarantino, with a 'big fucking asterisk' for Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.

films.title: Lady Bird
films.note: No. 5: the Gerwig pick over Little Women.

films.title: Mulholland Drive
films.note: No. 6: the David Lynch entry.

films.title: In the Mood for Love
films.note: No. 7: Wong Kar-wai; 'probably in the top ten.'

films.title: Parasite
films.note: No. 8: the Bong Joon-ho pick over Memories of Murder.

films.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
films.note: No. 9: 'the best action movie ever made,' per Soderbergh, per the show.

films.title: Marie Antoinette
films.note: No. 10: Sean's 'good magazine-editor pick,' over Lost in Translation.

films.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
films.note: No. 11: the Scorsese, over The Departed, Silence, and The Irishman.

films.title: The Royal Tenenbaums
films.note: No. 12: the Wes Anderson, locked in further after Gene Hackman's death.

films.title: Get Out
films.note: No. 13: the one horror movie on the list.

films.title: Moneyball
films.note: No. 14: 'patron saint of this podcast'; on the list no matter what.

films.title: Inside Llewyn Davis
films.note: No. 15: the Coen compromise between A Serious Man and No Country.

films.title: The Tree of Life
films.note: No. 16, swapped in for The Dark Knight late: 'the Tree of Life is better.'

films.title: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy
films.note: No. 17: the Will Ferrell pick over Superbad and Step Brothers.

films.title: Before Sunset
films.note: No. 18: a sequel, and 'one of the most romantic movies ever.'

films.title: Spirited Away
films.note: No. 19: the Miyazaki; 75th on the latest Sight & Sound poll.

films.title: Melancholia
films.note: No. 20: the only European director on the whole list, Lars von Trier.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - Fallout
films.note: No. 21: one of three action movies and three sequels here.

films.title: Something's Gotta Give
films.note: No. 22: the lone Nancy Meyers, over The Intern and It's Complicated.

films.title: Children of Men
films.note: No. 23: added at the buzzer once The Dark Knight was cut; Cuarón's slot.

films.title: The Handmaiden
films.note: No. 24: the Park Chan-wook; Sean may swap for Oldboy after a rewatch.

films.title: Michael Clayton
films.note: No. 25, a deliberate low opener: 'grab people up top and make them a little mad.'

referenced.group: Other director options weighed in passing
